channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Webinar: Why traditional email security is no longer enough
item.description: Modern phishing, business email compromise, and account takeover attacks increasingly exploit trusted identities and legitimate business workflows, making them harder for traditional email defenses to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers target Microsoft 365 accounts with 81 million login attempts
item.description: An aggressive password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments generated more than 81 million login attempts over a two-week period. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.description: The rules do not hand issuance to banks, but reserves, custody, audits and no-yield limits put financial infrastructure first. The post Taiwan’s new crypto law gives banks the first real stablecoin ad...

item.title: Ousaban Banking Trojan Targets Iberian Bank Users with Fake PDF Lures
item.description:
A Brazilian banking trojan called Ousaban is going after Windows users who bank in Spain and Portugal. Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs identified the campaign in May 2026.
It opens with a phishing PDF dis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adobe Patches 7 CVSS 10.0 Flaws in ColdFusion and Campaign Classic
item.description:
Adobe has released patches for multiple maximum-severity security flaws impacting Adobe ColdFusion and Adobe Campaign Classic.

item.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
item.description:
Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network.
An attacker within wireless ra...

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
item.description:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.
The flaw, tracked...
